notes:
Affiliation corrected with high confidence: the transcript's "COAP (Coalition for Positive Health Empowerment)" is a Whisper garble of COPE, the Coalition ON Positive Health Empowerment — a real nonprofit headquartered in Harlem with South Bronx focus, founded 2010 by Gloria Searson, EIN 27-2654975. The acronym is COPE (not COAP) and the preposition is "on" (not "for"). The testimony quote about ~1.2 million independent voters shut out of closed primaries fits the open-primaries advocacy that dominated the June 2026 COGE hearings.

The PERSONAL NAME could not be verified. COPE's public staff/board roster (Gloria Searson, Kwame Thompson, Gail Brown, Janice Sweeting, Escott Solomon, Sandee Myles, Pauline Edwards, Carlos Rosario; board: Kotler, Taylor, Copeland) contains no one resembling "Alan Cox," and no outreach worker named Alan/Allen Cox appears there. News coverage of COGE open-primaries testimony names John Opdycke (Open Primaries), Paul Rieckhoff, Dr. Jessie Fields and Nick Troiano — none a match. No public record links any Alan/Allen/Alon Cox to COPE, open-primaries advocacy, or the Queens hearing. This is most likely a frontline outreach worker testifying as a private individual with no findable public footprint. First name "Alan" is plausible; surname "Cox" is short and easily garbled by speech-to-text and is uncorroborated, so confidence is low. Kept the transcribed name rather than invent a correction.
